plainEnglish: Status messages—'Item added to cart', 'Search returned 12 results', a save confirmation—must be announced to screen readers without moving focus. Use an ARIA live region (aria-live, or role="status"/"alert") so assistive tech reads the update while the user keeps working.
whyItMatters: Screen reader users miss important feedback—like 'changes saved' or 'invalid coupon'—when status updates appear visually but aren't announced through a live region.
quickCheck: Trigger status updates (add to cart, search, save) and confirm they live in an aria-live region (role="status"/"alert"); verify a screen reader announces them without focus moving.
commonMistakes: Showing a toast or status text with no aria-live region, so screen readers stay silent.
Moving focus to the status message instead of announcing it via a live region.
Using role="alert" for non-urgent updates (it interrupts) instead of role="status".
Inserting the live region and its text at the same time, so the change isn't announced.

codeExamples.label: Status announced via a live region
codeExamples.code:
{/* Announced without moving focus */}
{statusMessage}

codeExamples.label: Status updates but is never announced
codeExamples.code:
{/* No live region — screen readers stay silent */}
{statusMessage}
